factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US long-bond yields hit highest since 2023; 30-year yields surged on inflation fears from Iran war oil shock. Rising nominal yields with inflation expectations moderating will push real yields higher, directly pressuring gold.

factors.name: Nominal US Treasury Yields
factors.summary: US 10y and 30y yields spiking to multi-year highs (30y highest since 2023); Japan 10y at 1996 highs (4.2%); global bond rout deepens. Steep yield curve attractive to capital, competing with gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Fed Tightening Bias
factors.summary: Iran war supply disruption fueling inflation fears; analysts like Yardeni warn Fed must tighten or lose control of rates. Heightened inflation concerns reinforce bets for higher interest rates, explicitly cited as reason gold fell below $4,550 per FXStreet.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD strengthening broadly; GBP/USD at 1.3300 (lowest since April 8), EUR/USD near 1.1600, AUD/USD under pressure at 0.7130. RBC Capital expects USD to drift higher; energy-insulated US status supporting dollar strength vs peers.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Geopolitical Risk Premium)
factors.summary: WTI at 2-week high (~$102.50+), Brent at ~$110; Iran war stalemate, UAE nuclear plant drone strike, Trump threats, Strait of Hormuz closure. Oil surge is inflationary, but markets interpreting as Fed-tightening driver, not gold safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Action
factors.summary: Asian equities mixed-to-weak (South Korea near limit-down, foreign investor selloff $13.2bn); yields spiking suppresses equity demand. Gold not benefiting from equity crash as much as USD/rates are repelling it.

factors.name: China Economic Data
factors.summary: April retail sales +0.2% y/y (worst since 2022, vs 2% expected); industrial production +4.1% vs 5.9% expected; house prices -3.5% y/y. Weak China demand reduces commodity bid; CNY weakening (PBOC fix 6.8435 vs 6.8086 estimate wider).

factors.name: Fed Policy Stance
factors.summary: No explicit FOMC decision in window, but Fed-speak environment is hawkish on inflation control; no new dovish pivot signals. Inflation surprise via energy costs shifting market expectations toward sustained higher rates.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows / Institutional Positioning
factors.summary: No explicit ETF flow data in news window; hedge fund positioning on sterling may distract from gold flows. India curbing silver imports (supportive for rupee, potentially gold demand headwind).

factors.name: Physical Demand Seasonality
factors.summary: No Indian wedding/festival or Chinese New Year seasonality signals in current news cycle. Late May timing not a strong seasonal tailwind for gold.

key_levels.support: $4,500–$4,480 (prior swing low test before further breakdown)
key_levels.resistance: $4,550–$4,600 (4h/daily pullback zone; break above needed to reverse SELL bias)

screening.narrative: Gold is under heavy pressure as the Iran war—far from triggering a safe-haven rally—is instead fueling an inflation shock that is driving nominal yields and real yields sharply higher. The collapse in China's April economic data (retail sales at 2022 lows, IP miss) and broad bond selloff across US, Japan, and EUR markets have made higher real rates the dominant narrative over geopolitical risk. The Fed's implicit hawkish bias on inflation control, combined with USD strength (energy-insulated US economy), is creating a headwind for gold despite oil at 2-week highs. Gold's decline below $4,550 reflects a regime shift: inflation-driven rate hikes are now pricing in faster than safe-haven demand can absorb.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI/PCE release (if scheduled next 24-72h; not explicitly in news but high-impact weekly stat)
RBA Minutes (May, Tuesday per calendar)
Japanese GDP Q1 (Tuesday)
UK Jobs (March, Tuesday)
Canadian Inflation (April, Tuesday)
FOMC decision/Powell remarks (if imminent; would be high-impact flip)
Further Trump–Iran escalation or Strait of Hormuz reopening news (could revert to safe-haven bid)
Additional China data or policy response to growth slowdown
screening.risk_warning: If Trump–Iran tensions escalate into military action (beyond rhetoric) or if Strait of Hormuz closes fully and oil spikes beyond $110–120, safe-haven bid could re-emerge and override rate narrative. Alternatively, if Fed signals a dovish pivot or inflation prints softer than expected, real yield pressure could ease and gold could rebound sharply. A breakdown below $4,480 could extend to $4,400, but a rebound above $4,600 would invalidate the SELL bias and suggest a bottom is forming.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (4h +0.38% off lows, but -0.31% on 24h). On PHASE_2 pullback, consider short entries on retest of $4,550 resistance or break below $4,500 support with tight stops above $4,560. Avoid chasing lower until a clear capitulation candle or till 4h/daily closes below $4,480 support. Monitor US bond yields and USD/JPY in real-time; if yields roll over or yen rallies, gold could find bids.

factors.name: Real Yields / Nominal Yields
factors.summary: US 30-year yield hit highest since 2023, global bond rout deepening per Bloomberg. Japanese 10-year yield hit 1996 high at 4.2% as fresh debt plans emerge. Rising nominal yields (without corresponding inflation offset) imply higher real yields, directionally bearish for gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Expectations
factors.summary: Multiple sources cite 'hawkish Fed bets' and 'rising Fed hike expectations' driven by persistent inflation concerns from Iran war oil shock. Yardeni Research warns Fed needs to 'catch up with bond markets or risk losing control' per Bloomberg. Market pricing in tightening bias, not easing.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD broadly firmer across pairs: GBP/USD at lowest since April 8, EUR/USD pressured near 1.1600, USD/JPY extended gains for sixth day to 158.90, USD/CAD above mid-1.3700s. RBC Capital expects dollar to drift higher citing yield advantage and energy insulation. Strong dollar is primary headwind for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices / Inflation Concerns
factors.summary: WTI at two-week high eyeing $102.50, Brent ~$110, driven by Iran war stalemate and drone strike on UAE nuclear facility. Jeff Currie warns US oil inventories approaching 'rock bottom' per Investinglive. Higher oil = inflation pressure = hawkish Fed = bearish gold, BUT geopolitical premium should support safe-haven bid. Net effect: inflation concern outweighing safe-haven in current phase.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: Iran war deadlock deepens: Trump warns Iran 'clock is ticking' and 'won't be anything left,' drone strike on UAE Barakah nuclear plant, Saudi Arabia also reports drone incidents, Strait of Hormuz remains blockaded, Trump situation room meeting Tuesday to discuss military option. Acute geopolitical stress should support gold safe-haven demand.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Asia-Pacific equities broadly lower, Dow futures drop per MarketWatch, South Korea KOSPI futures hit limit down triggering circuit breaker, Indonesia rupiah at record low, broad risk-off but USD capturing safe-haven flows instead of gold. Gold losing safe-haven competition to dollar.

factors.name: China Demand Proxy
factors.summary: China April data badly missed: Retail Sales +0.2% y/y vs 2.0% exp (weakest since 2022), Industrial Production +4.1% vs 5.9% exp, Fixed Asset Investment -1.6% vs +1.7% exp. Weak Chinese growth undermines physical gold demand outlook and broader commodity complex.

factors.name: Competing Safe-Havens
factors.summary: USD acting as primary safe-haven, capturing flows amid geopolitical stress. JPY weakening despite risk-off (USD/JPY 158.90), suggesting carry trade dynamics still intact. Gold losing relative appeal vs dollar in current regime.

factors.name: Gold Technicals / Momentum
factors.summary: Gold declined below $4,550 per FXStreet, currently $4,535.60, down -0.31% on 24h. Extended slump per Bloomberg as 'Hormuz quagmire keeps inflation fear high.' Price action shows loss of momentum from recent highs.

factors.name: Bond Market Signals
factors.summary: Global bond rout with yields at multi-year/multi-decade highs signals markets pricing persistent inflation and tighter policy ahead. Treasury selloff (30y at highest since 2023) is highly correlated with gold weakness when driven by inflation fears rather than growth optimism.

confirmation.narrative: Gold is caught in a bearish pincer: rising nominal/real yields driven by Iran war inflation fears are triggering hawkish Fed repricing, while a surging USD is capturing safe-haven flows that would normally accrue to gold. Despite acute geopolitical stress (UAE nuclear facility strike, Trump ultimatum to Iran), the dollar is winning the safe-haven competition. Weak Chinese economic data (retail sales at 2-year low) further undermines the demand outlook. The move lower is underway but not yet extended—gold has declined from recent highs but 24h move is only -0.31%.
catalysts_ahead: China April economic data already released (bearish—retail sales missed badly)
Trump situation room meeting Tuesday on Iran military options—potential escalation
Ongoing Iran war negotiations—breakthrough or breakdown would be major catalyst
Fed speaker calendar this week (any hawkish guidance would reinforce bearish bias)
Japan supplementary budget/debt issuance plans (further yield pressure)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Iran war breakthrough/Hormuz reopening causes oil crash and Fed pivot expectations, (2) Treasury yields reverse sharply lower on growth fears, (3) USD weakens significantly (DXY breakdown below key support), (4) Major geopolitical escalation (kinetic conflict resumption) triggers flight-to-quality into gold over dollar. Current setup assumes inflation > safe-haven in driver hierarchy.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is in progress (Phase 2) with gold already declining to $4,535 from higher levels. Wait for a bounce toward $4,560-4,580 resistance zone to consider shorts, or a break below $4,500 with volume for continuation. Avoid chasing here as geopolitical headlines can trigger sharp reversals.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Treasury yields surging amid inflation fears with bond selloff accelerating; higher nominal yields directly compress real yields, deeply negative for gold. FXStreet reports 'rates shock weighs on metals' with sharp corrections in gold and silver.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD extending broad-based strength on risk-off and Fed rate-hike repricing; multiple pairs weakening (EUR/USD at 6-week lows near 1.1620, GBP/USD below 1.3300 for 5+ weeks, USD/JPY retaking 159.00). Strong dollar directly competes with and suppresses gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Fed Policy Shift
factors.summary: Bond yields breaking March highs on inflation concerns; markets pricing out rate cuts and repricing in rate-hike odds as Fed abandons easing bias. FXStreet confirms traders have 'largely priced out US interest rate cuts this year, while expectations for a hike have risen.'

factors.name: Oil & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: WTI holding gains near $102.70 on third consecutive day, Brent supported, Strait of Hormuz closure extended into week 12 with Trump issuing fresh warnings. Higher oil = higher inflation expectations and potential safe-haven demand, but initial market reaction is risk-off, not risk-on.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equity Weakness
factors.summary: Broad risk-off underway: FTSE down, European stocks sliding, Asia-Pacific (Nikkei -1.08%, ASX -1.45%) falling. This is typically mixed for gold—safe-haven bid exists but is being overwhelmed by real-yield crush and USD strength.

factors.name: Gold ETF & Institutional Flows
factors.summary: No specific GLD/IAU data in news window; JP Morgan maintains $6,000 year-end target but cut 2026 average forecast to $5,243 from $5,708, suggesting downside pressure near-term before 2H recovery.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (BoE, BoJ, ECB)
factors.summary: BoE's Breeden says no rush to move next, but political uncertainty in UK (Starmer coup risk, Streeting bid). BoJ yields at 1996 highs as Japan issues fresh debt; yen weakening supports carry. ECB hawkish tone (Lagarde's worry) provides some EUR support but not enough to offset USD rally.

factors.name: Physical Demand Seasonality
factors.summary: No seasonal catalyst mentioned; Hajj/Eid on May 25 but too far out for immediate impact. Indian rupee at all-time lows (96.33 USD/INR) may suppress physical demand due to affordability.

key_levels.support: $4,480 (multi-month low set during Asian session Monday)

screening.narrative: Gold is in a deep correction phase driven by a forceful confluence of real-yield crush (Treasury yields breaking March highs on inflation panic), USD strength (DXY extending gains across all majors), and a shift in Fed expectations toward rate hikes rather than cuts. While geopolitical tensions (US-Iran stalemate, Strait of Hormuz closure) and elevated oil prices provide some inflation/safe-haven bid, the initial market reaction to these shocks is risk-off—equities falling, risk premium widening—but this is NOT translating into safe-haven flows into gold; instead, investors are dumping both equities AND gold in favor of higher real yields on Treasuries and a stronger dollar. The move lower in gold has already extended significantly from late last week; XAUUSD is testing multi-month lows near $4,480.
catalysts_ahead: US inflation data (CPI/Core CPI, PCE releases — dates TBD in next 7–10 days)
Fed speakers (Powell, Williams, Waller) on inflation/rate path
FOMC meeting (likely early June; currently market is pulling forward rate-hike odds)
US-Iran negotiations update or escalation (Trump's 'get moving FAST' threat)
China economic data recovery signals (April retail/industrial miss suggests weakness, but reopening narrative could flip sentiment)
G7 finance ministers outcome (bond rout, imbalances discussion)
screening.risk_warning: If oil prices spike sharply on renewed Iran military action (drone/missile strikes) or Hormuz closure worsens supply, risk-on sentiment could reverse, pulling equities and USD lower while gold safe-haven bid re-emerges—invalidating the SELL case. Conversely, if Fed speakers turn dovish or CPI prints cool unexpectedly, the real-yield compression could ease pressure and allow gold to bounce. A sudden risk-on rally in equities (e.g., China stimulus, US earnings beat) would also support gold despite higher yields.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended and late (already down >1% in 4h, multi-month lows reached). AVOID aggressive shorts here. Better entry would be a pullback/retest of $4,580–$4,600 resistance on any intraday bounce, or wait for 3-hour consolidation to reduce whipsaw risk. If yields stabilize or DXY breaks key support (1.07–1.08 level), reassess; until then, bias remains SELL on rallies, not on dips.

factors.name: Real Yields / Nominal Yields
factors.summary: Global bond rout underway with Treasury yields breaking March highs per Bloomberg; Japanese 10-year yield hits 1996 high; G7 finance chiefs meeting focused on bond ructions. Rising nominal yields and implied real yields are strongly bearish for gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: Markets have largely priced out US rate cuts this year with expectations for a Fed rate hike rising per multiple sources; Fed abandoning easing bias as inflation worries mount. Hawkish repricing is bearish gold.

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY)
factors.summary: USD buying remains unabated per FXStreet; USD/JPY retakes 159.00 (multi-week high), EUR/USD at 6-week low near 1.1620, USD/CHF near highest since April 30. Strong dollar is major headwind for gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Oil prices at $102.70 WTI with Brent elevated on Iran war; Bloomberg headline 'Why the Price of Oil, Beef, Electricity, and Everything Else Makes No Sense' signals broad inflation shock; rising oil prices promoting global inflation expectations further per FXStreet.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran War)
factors.summary: Iran war day 80 with Strait of Hormuz closure continuing; Trump warns Iran 'clock is ticking'; drone attacks on UAE/Saudi Arabia; talks through Pakistani mediation ongoing but stalled. Geopolitical risk typically bullish gold but being overwhelmed by rates shock per OCBC: 'higher yields and stronger Dollar overwhelmed safe-haven demand.'

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: WTI near $102.50, Brent elevated with supply disruptions from Strait of Hormuz closure; Goldman warns Europe faces summer jet fuel squeeze with 'demand rationing pricing' ahead. Higher oil feeds inflation expectations, pressures Fed hawkish, indirectly bearish for gold via yields.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Broad risk-off: Asia-Pacific markets fell, European stocks slide, Nikkei -1.08%, ASX -1.45%, FTSE lower; bond yields surging 'a major pain point for equities' per Investinglive. Risk-off typically bullish gold but current regime shows safe-haven bid going to USD instead.

factors.name: China Demand / Economic Data
factors.summary: China April retail sales +0.2% vs 2.0% expected (weakest since 2022), industrial production +4.1% vs 5.9% expected per NBS; data 'misses badly' signaling weak domestic demand. China is major physical gold buyer; weak economy reduces structural demand.

factors.name: Gold Technical / Flows
factors.summary: OCBC reports 'sharp corrections in Gold and Silver as higher yields and stronger Dollar overwhelmed safe-haven demand'; gold set multi-month low around $4,480 during Asian session. Move already underway from previous highs.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No specific recent data in news window on PBoC, RBI, or other central bank purchases; this structural bid remains but not mentioned as offsetting current bearish factors.

factors.name: Competing Safe Havens
factors.summary: USD acting as primary safe haven in current regime rather than gold; safe-haven flows competing with gold are going to greenback. Swiss franc also flatlines near multi-week low vs USD.

confirmation.narrative: Gold faces a 'rates shock' as the dominant narrative, with the global bond rout and Fed rate hike repricing overwhelming traditional geopolitical safe-haven bids from the ongoing Iran war (day 80). Despite extreme oil supply disruption and Middle East tensions, OCBC explicitly notes that 'higher yields and stronger Dollar overwhelmed safe-haven demand.' The USD is capturing safe-haven flows instead of gold, while surging real yields (10-year JGB at 1996 highs, Treasuries breaking March peaks) create the worst macro backdrop for gold. China's dismal April data (retail sales +0.2% vs 2% expected) adds structural demand concerns.
catalysts_ahead: G7 finance ministers meeting outcomes (currently underway in Paris, focused on bond market volatility)
Any breakthrough or escalation in US-Iran negotiations via Pakistani mediation
Further Fed speakers clarifying rate hike probabilities
Weekly oil inventory data and Strait of Hormuz transit developments
China policy response to weak April economic data
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) US-Iran breakthrough announced (Strait of Hormuz reopening would collapse oil premium and inflation expectations), (2) Fed speakers push back hard against rate hike pricing, signaling cuts still on table, (3) Treasury yields reverse sharply lower on recession fears, (4) USD weakens materially on risk-on rotation. Gold already down to $4,480 intraday low—move is Phase 2, not fresh.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway with gold testing $4,480 multi-month low; wait for retest of $4,520-4,540 resistance zone before adding short exposure, or confirmation of break below $4,480 for continuation.

reason: FLIP + CONFIRMED: BEARISH → SELL, both models conf ≥7

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Real yields have surged sharply; multiple sources cite 'real yields surged' as the primary headwind for gold Friday into Monday. Higher real yields are the single strongest inverse correlate with gold prices.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: Markets pricing in Fed hikes amid inflation concerns; Bloomberg reports 'worries of Fed rate hikes' are pressuring gold. No recent dovish Fed surprise; stance remains hawkish-biased relative to market expectations.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY eased to 99.10 on Iran deal optimism (Reuters, FXStreet 10:10), but the underlying driver is higher US yields supporting structural USD strength per DBS commentary. USD strength is BEARISH for gold.

factors.name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
factors.summary: Nominal yields remain elevated after Friday's surge to multi-year highs; news reports 'Treasury yields are little changed' and strategists warn of potential 5% yields ahead (Ed Yardeni, MarketWatch). Higher nominal yields reduce gold's appeal.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran)
factors.summary: Mixed headlines: Trump warned 'clock is ticking' Sunday (hawkish), but Monday brought news of US-Iran talks resuming via Oman mediation and potential temporary sanctions waiver proposal. De-escalation chatter is lifting risk sentiment and weakening safe-haven demand.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent)
factors.summary: Brent reached ~$110 with supply-side support from Iran war disruptions and IEA warnings of rapid inventory drawdown. Higher oil = inflation pressure, which typically bullish for gold medium-term, but currently overshadowed by real yield damage.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures down 0.4% (Bloomberg 11:41), but equities showing resilience despite yield/oil pressure. No panic VIX spike; sentiment cautious rather than deeply risk-off, reducing safe-haven tailwind for gold.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Physical Demand
factors.summary: No material ETF flow data in news window. India tightening gold import curbs (Bloomberg 11:54), creating minor headwind for demand, but not a dominant factor in current price action.

factors.name: Prior Bias Alignment
factors.summary: 2026-05-18 03:41 read was SELL 7/10, then 08:01 SELL 7/10. Current sell bias is continuation, increasing reliability over fresh reversal trade.

factors.name: Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Gold has fallen from $4,770 (4-day selloff to current $4,553), losing ~$220 or 4.6%. Current +0.14% 4h and +0.20% 24h suggests stabilization attempt, but move is extended. Stabilizing above $4,500 per FXStreet 11:26.

key_levels.support: 4500 (psychological, stabilizing near this level), 4450 (recent low area before bounce)

screening.narrative: Gold is in a sustained downtrend driven by surging real yields (10y TIPS) and elevated nominal Treasury yields post-Friday's bond selloff, with the Fed maintaining a hawkish bias on inflation concerns. While geopolitical tensions (Iran) and elevated oil prices (~$110 Brent) provide medium-term support, they are currently outweighed by the deflationary impulse of higher rates and stronger USD. The move from $4,770 to $4,553 represents a 4.6% decline over 4 days (PHASE_2 extended), and current stabilization suggests exhaustion, but momentum remains down. Iran de-escalation chatter on Monday is providing temporary relief and risk-on sentiment, but this is fragile.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI/Core CPI release (if scheduled within 24-72h; not explicitly dated in news)
FOMC meeting/Powell testimony (timing unclear; significant upcoming)
Further Iran-US negotiations outcome (binary de-escalation vs escalation)
US Treasury yield direction (any move >10 bps could re-anchor gold)
Oil supply data (IEA weekly report, OPEC news)
US economic data (NFP, ISM if scheduled)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Real yields collapse sharply on recession/deflationary shock (supporting gold); (2) Fed pivots dovish unexpectedly (bullish gold, bearish USD); (3) Iran escalation triggers major geopolitical crisis and flight-to-safety (gold rallies hard despite yields); (4) US inflation prints cold, reducing Fed hike expectations; (5) DXY breaks below 98.5 on risk-on reversal.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended (Phase 2/3); avoid chasing shorts. On retest of $4,550–$4,580 resistance with failed break above, consider short entry to $4,500–$4,480 support. Alternatively, wait for real yield spike confirmation or Fed hawkish signal before re-entering. Safe-haven dip buyers should wait for $4,450 or below on escalation risk before covering.
